name: Dental Records & Consent (DCNZ aligned)
description: Reference policy aligned with the Dental Council of New Zealand Standards Framework and Practice Standards.

data.text: Records are clear, contemporaneous, and detailed enough for another dental practitioner to continue care.
Patient records are retained for at least 10 years from the date of last contact.
Tooth charting uses an explicit notation system (FDI or Universal); restorations, caries, mobility, and missing teeth are recorded for every full exam.
Periodontal status is recorded at every full exam — screening or full periodontal charting as clinically indicated.
Informed consent is documented for treatment plans, especially extractions, endodontics, sedation, and prostheses.
Each entry identifies the treating clinician; corrections by addendum only.

clauses.title: Records sufficient for handover
clauses.body: You must keep records detailed enough for another dental practitioner to continue care.

clauses.title: 10-year retention
clauses.body: You must retain patient records for at least 10 years from last contact.

clauses.title: Tooth charting recorded
clauses.body: Every full exam must include tooth charting using an explicit notation system.

clauses.title: Periodontal status recorded
clauses.body: Every full exam must record periodontal status (screening or full charting).

clauses.title: Treatment consent documented
clauses.body: You must document informed consent for treatment plans, especially invasive procedures.

clauses.title: Clinician sign-off identified
clauses.body: Each entry must identify the treating clinician; corrections by addendum only.
